mission.title: Install and Configure Oscal Compass on Kubernetes
mission.description: Compliance Trestle is a tooling platform designed to manage compliance as code using NIST's OSCAL standard. Installing it allows you to create, validate, and govern compliance documentation efficiently within your CI/CD pipelines.

steps.title: Add the Compliance Trestle Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Compliance Trestle Helm repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add compliance-trestle https://oscal-compass.github.io/compliance-trestle
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Compliance Trestle using Helm
steps.description:
Install Compliance Trestle in a dedicated namespace (e.g., 'compliance') with the specified version.
```bash
helm install trestle compliance-trestle/compliance-trestle --namespace compliance --create-namespace --version v3.12.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Compliance Trestle pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace compliance
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Compliance Trestle deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/trestle --namespace compliance --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the Compliance Trestle service to access it via your browser.
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/trestle --namespace compliance 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Compliance Trestle pods running in the 'compliance' namespace, and you will be able to access the application via http://localhost:8080. Resource limits will be applied to the deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add compliance-trestle https://oscal-compass.github.io/compliance-trestle
helm repo update
helm install trestle compliance-trestle/compliance-trestle --namespace compliance --create-namespace --version v3.12.0

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description: Uninstall the Compliance Trestle release from the Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description: Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Compliance Trestle.

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description: Delete the 'compliance' namespace and verify that all resources have been cleaned up.

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description: Before upgrading, back up the current state of the Compliance Trestle release.

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description: Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Compliance Trestle release to the latest version.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description: Check if the Compliance Trestle pods are running the new version successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description: This issue occurs when pods repeatedly crash. Check the logs of the pod to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description: If the service is not accessible, ensure that the port forwarding is set up correctly and the service is running.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description: If the pods are failing due to resource limits, consider increasing the resource limits set for the deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter an error stating the Helm release is not found, verify that you are using the correct namespace and release name.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
